content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


March 1, 2004


Lance H. Olson
Olson, Hagel & Fishburn
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 1425
Sacramento, CA 95814-4602

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-04-010

Dear Mr. Olson:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of State Insurance Commissioner John Garamendi for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your question is general in nature and without regard to any identified expenditure we are providing informal assistance.²

QUESTION

May Insurance Commissioner Garamendi use funds raised into his 2006 reelection committee to pay for current on-going expenses associated with holding the office of Insurance Commissioner?

CONCLUSION

Yes.

FACTS

Commissioner Garamendi has two controlled committees. The Garamendi Committee and Garamendi 2006. The Garamendi Committee was formed in connection with Mr. Garamendi's election in 2002 to his current position. This committee has no net

_______________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear in Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice (Government Code § 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. § 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)

File No. I-04-010
Page No. 2

debts. The Garamendi Committee has some funds on hand, but not enough to cover
anticipated expenses associated with holding the office of Insurance Commissioner
during the remaining months of the current term of office. The Garamendi 2006
committee was formed in connection with his planned re-election efforts and has begun
raising funds in compliance with applicable contribution limits.

Mr. Garamendi understands that the Commission's recent adoption of regulation
18531.61 will preclude him from raising any funds into the Garamendi Committee
beginning January 24, 2004. He would like to use funds raised into his Garamendi 2006
committee to pay for current on-going expenses associated with holding the office of
Insurance Commissioner.

ANALYSIS

Regulation 18525 addresses incumbent candidates' election expenses and
officeholder expenses. Although regulation 18525 (copy enclosed) sets forth the kinds of
expenditures that must be made from accounts for election to future office by incumbent
elected officers, officeholder expenses are not listed among them. Subdivision (b) of that
regulation deals with expenditures not specifically listed, and states, in pertinent part:

"(b) An incumbent elected officer may make expenditures
for purposes not enumerated in subdivision (a) from either the
campaign bank account established pursuant to Government Code
Section 85201 for election to the incumbent term of office or from a
campaign bank account established pursuant to Government Code
Section 85201 for election to a future term of office. This section
shall not be construed to permit an incumbent elected officer to
make expenditures from any campaign bank account for expenses
other than those associated with his or her election to the specific
office for which the account was established and expenses
associated with holding that office."

Thus, pursuant to regulation 18525, subdivision (b), Insurance Commissioner
Garamendi may use his 2006 reelection account to pay for officeholder expenses
associated with holding that office. (See also, Hiltachk Advice Letter, A-04-006; Danner
Advice Letter, No. A-96-109.)

File No. I-04-010
Page No. 3

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: C. Scott Tacher
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures
